evals.prompt: Automate going to https://books.toscrape.com and extract all book titles and prices from the first page
evals.expected_output: A class-based Python script using playwright.sync_api that navigates to the URL, extracts book titles and prices, and saves to CSV
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block (GOAL, TASK PLAN, DONE WHEN) before any browser action
Agent uses SKIP or LIGHT recon (not FULL) — this is a single-page static task
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation (not browser_take_screenshot for every step)
Script is saved to ./playwright-scripts/ directory
Script uses class-based structure with setup/teardown/step methods
Script uses playwright.sync_api, NOT requests or BeautifulSoup
Script has argparse with --headed, --verbose, --url flags
Script uses logging module, not print statements
Script saves data output as CSV (tabular data)
Agent builds script incrementally via MCP (not writing full script upfront)
Script uses wait_until='domcontentloaded' (not 'networkidle')
Script does not use time.sleep()
If pagination is explored, agent generalizes to a Python loop after 2 pages (Pattern Recognition)

evals.prompt: Write a playwright script to log into https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login with username 'tomsmith' and password 'SuperSecretPassword!', then verify the success message
evals.expected_output: A class-based Python script that logs in using env vars for credentials and verifies success
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block (GOAL, TASK PLAN, DONE WHEN) before any browser action
Agent uses LIGHT or FULL recon, recognizing auth is involved
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation tool
Script is saved to ./playwright-scripts/ directory
Script uses class-based structure with setup/teardown/step methods
Credentials use os.environ with NO fallback defaults
Script has argparse with --headed, --verbose, --url flags
Script uses logging module, not print statements
Script uses get_by_role, get_by_label, or get_by_text for selectors (not just CSS)
Script verifies the success message after login
Script catches PlaywrightTimeout with contextual error info
Agent marks sub-tasks complete in TASK PLAN as it progresses
Agent stops after DONE WHEN is met (does not explore additional pages post-login)

evals.expected_output: A class-based Python script that downloads files using Playwright's download handling
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block (GOAL, TASK PLAN, DONE WHEN) before any browser action
Agent uses SKIP or LIGHT recon — this is a simple download task
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation tool
Script is saved to ./playwright-scripts/ directory
Script uses class-based structure with setup/teardown/step methods
Script uses accept_downloads=True on browser context (set in setup())
Script uses page.expect_download() before clicking download link
Script uses download.save_as() to persist the file
Script has argparse with --headed, --verbose, --url flags
Script uses logging module, not print statements
Script has error handling for download failures
Agent builds script incrementally via MCP
Agent uses Layered Debug (Quick Check first) if any step fails

evals.prompt: Scrape all book titles from all 50 pages of https://books.toscrape.com
evals.expected_output: A class-based Python script that scrapes all pages using a pagination loop, NOT by visiting all 50 pages via MCP
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block before any browser action
Agent uses SKIP recon (static site, structure is obvious)
Agent visits page 1 and page 2 via MCP to understand pagination pattern
Agent applies Pattern Recognition — generalizes to a Python loop after 2 iterations
Agent does NOT visit all 50 pages via MCP tools
Script contains a while/for loop for pagination
Script uses browser_snapshot as primary observation (not screenshots for every page)
Script saves all titles to CSV
Script uses wait_until='domcontentloaded'
Agent stops when DONE WHEN criteria are met

evals.prompt: Fill out the multi-step form at https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/dynamic_controls and verify the result
evals.expected_output: A class-based script handling dynamic content with explicit waits
expectations: Agent writes a Goal Lock block before any browser action
Agent uses LIGHT recon to understand the form structure
Agent uses browser_snapshot as primary observation
Script uses page.wait_for_selector() for dynamic elements (not networkidle)
If action fails, agent uses Quick Check first (not full 5-step investigation)
After any debug fix, agent re-reads Goal Lock before continuing (GOAL CHECK)
Agent does not explore unrelated pages on the site
Script uses get_by_role or get_by_text selectors

evals.prompt: Automate navigating to https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/dynamic_loading/1 — click Start, wait for the hidden element to appear, and extract the displayed text
evals.expected_output: A class-based Python script that handles dynamic loading with explicit waits and Layered Debug if the element isn't immediately available
expectations: Agent writes Goal Lock before any browser action
Agent uses browser_snapshot after clicking Start to verify loading state
Script uses page.wait_for_selector() to wait for the dynamically loaded element
If element not found, agent applies Quick Check first (not Full Investigation)
Script does NOT use time.sleep() to wait for loading
Script captures the loaded text and logs it
Script uses class-based structure with setup/teardown/step methods
Agent marks sub-tasks as complete in TASK PLAN

evals.prompt: Write a playwright script that: 1) logs into https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login with credentials from env vars, 2) navigates to /secure, 3) verifies the secure area content, 4) logs out, 5) verifies redirect back to login page
evals.expected_output: A class-based script with separate step methods for login, verify, logout, and verify-logout
expectations: Agent writes Goal Lock with all 4+ sub-tasks before any browser action
Agent uses FULL recon (multi-page flow with auth)
Script has separate step_NN methods for each phase (login, verify-secure, logout, verify-redirect)
Credentials use os.environ with NO fallback defaults
Script verifies success at each step (not just login)
Agent marks sub-tasks complete in sequence
Agent stops when DONE WHEN is met (does not explore other pages)
Script uses class-based structure with setup/teardown
Script has argparse with --headed, --verbose, --url flags

evals.prompt: Automate clicking the 'Challenging DOM' button at https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/challenging_dom and extract the full table data
evals.expected_output: A class-based script that handles non-standard selectors, demonstrating the Layered Debug protocol
expectations: Agent writes Goal Lock before any browser action
If initial selector fails, agent uses Quick Check first (not Full Investigation)
If Quick Check fails, agent escalates to Full Investigation (screenshot + console + evaluate)
Agent uses browser_evaluate to inspect DOM structure when standard selectors fail
Script uses robust selectors discovered through the debug process
Agent does NOT ask user for help before attempting autonomous debug
Script saves extracted table data as CSV
Script uses class-based structure with setup/teardown/step methods

evals.prompt: Automate clicking the 'Submit' button on https://example.com/dashboard where three distinct Submit buttons exist (one per form card) and extract the resulting confirmation text
evals.expected_output: Dispatcher skill routes to domain-playwright-lead. Agent detects selector ambiguity, emits NEEDS_MENTOR with an options block, and does NOT guess a button.
expectations: Main thread (skill) spawns domain-playwright-lead with a session_id
Agent writes session state to .claude/agent-memory/domain-playwright-lead/sessions/<id>/state.json
On ambiguity (multiple matching elements), agent returns a NEEDS_MENTOR block (does not pick arbitrarily)
NEEDS_MENTOR block contains session_id, checkpoint, context, question, options (A/B/…), blockers
Mentor auto-answers from original user goal + prior turns when inferable
Agent resumes with RESUME session <id>. Mentor answer: ... and continues from checkpoint
Agent does not hardcode any credential, URL fragment, or selector guess
Round-trip ceiling honored: max 10 mentor round-trips OR 3 deadlocks (whichever first) then ESCALATE_USER

evals.prompt: Log into the internal reporting tool and download yesterday's revenue report
evals.expected_output: Agent detects missing credentials + ambiguous 'internal reporting tool' target and consults mentor instead of hardcoding defaults or guessing a URL.
expectations: Main thread (skill) spawns domain-playwright-lead with a session_id
Agent does NOT hardcode credentials, not even as 'fallback defaults'
Agent emits NEEDS_MENTOR asking for (a) tool URL, (b) credential env var names, (c) report selector/filter
If the mentor's auto-answer cannot resolve the same blocker-category after 3 attempts, mentor escalates to user
Deadlock is counted by (checkpoint + blocker-category), not raw question text
Counter is owned by main thread, not the agent's state.json
On ESCALATE_USER, agent returns diagnostic block with session_id, history, and last checkpoint
No browser action happens until the question is answered
